purpose: A runtime-neutral lifecycle boundary for Pi, oh-my-pi, Claude, Codex, and future Agent executors.

input_contract.secret_policy: Secrets remain in managed credential references and never enter prompts, runtime args, receipts, or output payloads.
input_contract.scope_rule: Runtime receives only the Work Node scope and the minimum sanitized context required for the next action.

output_contract.result_rule: A runtime result is a candidate artifact or typed subagent result; it cannot be a verification or adoption decision.

capabilities.nested_call_rule: Every nested kernel, subagent, reviewer, or RPC callback reuses the active Grant and emits a new Action Receipt.

budget.fallback_rule: Runtime fallback chains cannot expand budget, scope, model policy, or risk ceiling.

invariants: Changing the runtime adapter never changes work_node_id, grant_id, trace_id, scope, or verification result.
The runtime cannot mark a Work Node verified or accepted.
A runtime version drift resets trust and requires a new Broker admission decision.
A runtime may report candidate output, but only an independent verifier can satisfy acceptance criteria.
